title: 10 best herbal essential oils for nausea
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for nausea are concentrated plant extracts that have anti-inflammatory and calming properties, helping to alleviate and prevent nausea. The best herbal essential oils for nausea include Zingiber officinale, Foeniculum vulgare, Mentha x piperita, Piper nigrum, Ginkgo biloba, Cinnamomum verum, Cinchona officinalis, Ammi majus, Glycyrrhiza glabra, and Piper methysticum. If left untreated, nausea can lead to dehydration, fatigue, and a decrease in productivity, negatively impacting daily life, relationships, and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with nausea because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the stomach lining and reduce digestive discomfort. The oil's active compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to bind to receptors in the brain, suppressing the symptoms of nausea and vomiting. Additionally, ginger's aroma can also stimulate digestion and relieve bloating, further alleviating queasy feelings and providing fast relief from morning sickness and motion-related nausea.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's essential oil contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate nausea.
Digestive stimulants: The oil has natural digestive stimulants that can help stimulate digestion, relieve bloating, and calm the stomach muscles, reducing nausea and vomiting.

Antiemetic properties: Herbal ginger essential oil has been shown to have antiemetic properties, which means it can help prevent or reduce the frequency and severity of nausea, vomiting, and other symptoms associated with motion sickness, morning sickness, and chemotherapy-induced nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of volatile compounds that have been shown to alleviate nausea and vomiting.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, or underground stem, contains a high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have potent anti-nausea properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots of zingiber officinale at dawn when they are most potent and store them in a cool dry place for up to two weeks.
Wash the harvested ginger roots thoroughly under cold running water removing any dirt or debris from their surfaces.
Dry the washed ginger roots by spreading them out on paper towels or clean cloth towels allowing excess moisture to evaporate completely.
Steam distill the dried ginger roots using a still apparatus with 20 kilograms of roots per liter of distilled water for three hours at atmospheric pressure.

Collect and filter the resulting essential oil using cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding any impurities and storing the pure zingiber officinale essential oil in dark glass bottles tightly sealed at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava essential oils helps with nausea because of its natural anxiolytic properties, which help to reduce feelings of anxiety and stress that often contribute to nausea. The oil's calming effects also aid in soothing the stomach muscles, reducing spasms and cramps that can exacerbate nausea. Additionally, kava essential oils have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract that can cause nausea and discomfort. Overall, incorporating kava essential oils into a wellness routine can provide natural relief from nausea symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Kava essential oil has anxiolytic properties, which helps to reduce anxiety and stress-related nausea by promoting relaxation and calming the mind.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in kava essential oil can help to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can contribute to nausea and alleviate symptoms such as bloating and discomfort.
Gastroprotective properties: Kava essential oil has been shown to protect the stomach from damage caused by acid reflux and other digestive issues, which can help to prevent nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the kava plant are rich in alkaloids that help to calm the digestive system and relieve nausea symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Kava rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including nausea, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and relax muscle contractions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of piper methysticum and rinse them thoroughly under running water.
Combine 10g of dried piper methysticum leaves with 200ml of high-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 14 days at room temperature allowing it to infuse slowly.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard solids after 24 hours.
Transfer the resulting liquid to a dark glass bottle and store it away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with nausea because of its calming and soothing properties. When inhaled, it can help to slow down digestion and reduce symptoms such as bloating and discomfort. Additionally, peppermint's natural antispasmodic properties can help to relax the stomach muscles, alleviating feelings of queasiness and reducing the frequency of vomiting. This makes herbal peppermint essential oil a popular natural remedy for nausea relief during pregnancy, motion sickness, and other situations where digestive upset is common.
remedy_properties: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds, particularly menthol and menthone, have natural anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that can help alleviate nausea and vomiting by reducing stomach inflammation and discomfort.

Volatile Oils: The volatile oils in peppermint essential oil, such as limonene and beta-pinene, have been shown to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion, including nausea, by increasing the movement of food through the digestive system.

Methanol: Peppermint essential oil's methanol content has natural antispasmodic properties that can help relax the stomach muscles, reducing spasms and cramps that often contribute to nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of peppermint contain menthol and other bioactive compounds that help to calm digestive issues and reduce nausea.
Leaves: The leaves of peppermint contain a high concentration of menthone, which is responsible for its anti-nausea properties and ability to ease digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the peppermint leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 3 hours.
Crush the dried peppermint leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix one cup of the powdered peppermint with two cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids after 14 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon essential oils helps with nausea because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate digestive discomfort. The oil's warming and calming effects can also soothe an upset stomach and ease queasiness. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon has been traditionally used to treat various gastrointestinal issues, including indigestion and bloating. By inhaling or applying topically, the essential oil can provide rapid relief from nausea symptoms, promoting a sense of relaxation and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Relaxes digestive muscles, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to nausea and vomiting.
Carminative property: Helps to release trapped gas in the digestive system, alleviating discomfort and bloating associated with nausea.
Anti-inflammatory property: Soothes inflammation in the digestive tract, which may help alleviate nausea and vomiting caused by underlying inflammatory conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Ceylon cinnamon plant contain compounds like cinnamic acid and cinnamaldehyde, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate nausea symptoms.

Barks: The barks of the Ceylon cinnamon plant are rich in phenolic compounds like cinnamic acid and its derivatives, which have been shown to exhibit anti-emetic properties, making them effective for treating nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cinnamomum verum bark from mature trees in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested bark in a warm, well-ventilated area for at least 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill 100 grams of dried cinnamomum verum bark with 500 milliliters of distilled water for 4 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation residues by cooling the vapor condensate to a temperature below 20°c and collecting the resulting oil.
Filter the obtained essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and sediment, yielding approximately 10 milliliters of cinnamomum verum essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with nausea because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the digestive system and ease stomach discomfort. The oil's anise-like scent also stimulates digestion and relieves bloating, making it a natural remedy for alleviating nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness, pregnancy, or chemotherapy. Additionally, fennel essential oil has been shown to stimulate gastric motility, helping food move through the digestive system more efficiently and reducing feelings of queasiness.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Fennel essential oil can stimulate digestive enzymes, reducing symptoms of nausea by promoting healthy digestion and relieving cramps.
Antispasmodic: The oil's antispasmodic properties help to relax smooth muscle contractions in the stomach and intestines, alleviating nausea and vomiting caused by spasms or irritations.
Anti-inflammatory: Fennel essential oil's anti-inflammatory effects can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which may contribute to nausea and other symptoms, such as bloating or cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the fennel plant are rich in volatile oils, which help to alleviate nausea and digestive issues by soothing the stomach and intestines.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a unique compound called anethole, which has been shown to have anti-nausea properties and can help to calm the stomach and reduce nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh foeniculum vulgare plant leaves and flowers in the early morning to ensure peak oil content.
Dry the harvested plant material for at least 7 days in a warm dark place to reduce moisture levels.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a copper alembic still with 30% water and 70% plant material for 1 hour.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distilled steam condensate after 10 minutes of standing time.
Filter the collected essential oil through activated charcoal to remove impurities and sediment before bottling it in dark glass containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with nausea because of its natural calming and soothing properties. The oil contains apigenin, an antioxidant that binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety-related nausea. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds help to ease digestive discomfort and calm stomach cramps, alleviating symptoms of nausea and vomiting. When inhaled or applied topically, chamomile essential oil can quickly reduce nausea and promote feelings of calmness and serenity.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm digestive tract inflammation, which can contribute to nausea and stomach upset.
Anxiolytic: The anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties of chamomile essential oil help to calm the mind and body, reducing feelings of stress and anxiety that can exacerbate nausea.
Digestive soothing: Chamomile's digestive-soothing properties have a gentle calming effect on the stomach, which can help to reduce nausea and alleviate symptoms such as bloating and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of chamomile contain apigenin, a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in soothing digestive issues and relieving nausea.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to calm the stomach and alleviate symptoms of nausea due to their gentle sedative effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality matricaria chamomilla flowers at full bloom in the morning ensuring maximum potency.
Dry the flowers using a warm air circulation method or shade drying for 24 to 48 hours.
Steam distill the dried flowers using a steam distillation apparatus and collect the essential oil within 4 hours of distillation.
Mix the essential oil with a carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil at a ratio of 10 drops per milliliter.
Store the finished essential oil in dark glass bottles away from direct sunlight and heat for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Coriander essential oils helps with nausea because of its natural ability to calm the digestive system. The oil's active compounds, such as linalool and geranyl acetate, have a soothing effect on the stomach muscles, reducing spasms and inflammation that can contribute to nausea. Additionally, coriander essential oils have a calming aroma that can help reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers of nausea. By promoting relaxation and easing digestive discomfort, coriander essential oils provide natural relief from nausea, making it an effective remedy for individuals experiencing morning sickness or motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Coriander oil helps to expel gas from the digestive tract, reducing nausea and discomfort associated with indigestion and bloating.
Antispasmodic property: Coriander oil's ability to relax smooth muscle tissue in the stomach and intestines can help alleviate nausea and vomiting caused by spasms or inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory property: Coriander oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to nausea and discomfort, particularly during menstrual periods or with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the coriander plant are used to create essential oils that have been shown to help alleviate nausea and vomiting due to their high concentration of sesquiterpenes.
Seeds: Coriander seeds contain a compound called citronellal, which has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe stomach upset and alleviate nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh coriander leaves at dawn when their oil content is highest.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer on paper towels for 7 days.
Use a coffee grinder or spice grinder to grind 20g of dried coriander into fine powder.
Steam distill the ground coriander in a double boiler setup with 100ml water at 60°c for 2 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth and store it in dark glass bottles for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cumin essential oils helps with nausea because of its unique properties that stimulate digestion and alleviate stomach discomfort. The oil's active compounds, such as cuminaldehyde and linoleic acid, have natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help to soothe the digestive tract, reducing nausea and vomiting symptoms. Additionally, cumin essential oil's warm and spicy aroma can also help to calm the mind and body, further alleviating feelings of queasiness and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Cumin essential oil's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting caused by digestive issues.

Carminative properties: The carminative compounds in cumin essential oil help expel gas and relieve bloating and cramping in the stomach, which can alleviate nausea and discomfort associated with indigestion.

Digestive stimulant properties: Cumin essential oil's digestive stimulants increase gut motility and improve digestion, reducing symptoms of nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain caused by slow digestion or food intolerance.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the cumin plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing nausea and vomiting symptoms.
Seeds: Cumin seeds contain a high concentration of volatile oils that have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including nausea and stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cuminum cyminum seeds, ensuring they are free of impurities and pests.
Dry the seeds in a well-ventilated area for at least two weeks, stirring occasionally, to reduce moisture levels.
Crush the dried seeds into smaller pieces using a mortar and pestle, taking about 30 minutes to complete.
Steam distill the crushed seeds in a glass still with water for approximately four hours, collecting the resulting essential oil.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter or paper towel to remove any impurities, then store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with nausea because of its calming and soothing properties. The oil's fragrance has been shown to ease digestive discomfort, reducing symptoms of motion sickness, morning sickness, and chemotherapy-induced nausea. When inhaled, lavender oil can help regulate the body's natural balance, alleviating feelings of queasiness and promoting relaxation. Additionally, the oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe the stomach lining, further reducing nausea and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Lavender oil's anti-inflammatory effects can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which may contribute to nausea and alleviate symptoms.
Anxiolytic property: The calming and relaxing effects of lavender oil can help reduce anxiety, a common trigger for nausea, by promoting relaxation and reducing stress hormones like cortisol.
Antiemetic property: Lavender oil's natural ability to soothe the stomach and digestive system may help alleviate nausea and vomiting by regulating gut motility and reducing spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant are valued for their calming properties, which can help alleviate nausea and promote relaxation.
Flowers: Lavender flowers contain fragrant oil with a soothing effect on the digestive system, making them effective in treating nausea and promoting a sense of calm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavender flowers at dawn when they are fully opened for maximum oil content.
Dry the harvested lavender flowers in small batches for 24-48 hours using low-temperature oven.
Combine 100g of dried lavender flowers with 400ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or jojoba oil.
Steep the mixture for 6-8 weeks, shaking the container daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard solids, then store the resulting essential oil in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cloves essential oils helps with nausea because of its unique properties that can calm the stomach and alleviate discomfort. The oil contains a compound called eugenol, which has natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help to ease digestive issues. When inhaled or applied topically, clove oil can help to reduce nausea and vomiting by soothing the stomach muscles and reducing inflammation. Additionally, its calming aroma can also help to relax the mind and body, further alleviating symptoms of nausea.
remedy_properties: Antiemetic property: Clove essential oil has been shown to have anti-nausea effects, helping to reduce vomiting and nausea by acting on the brain's chemoreceptor trigger zone.
Digestive stimulant property: The essential oil stimulates digestion and relieves symptoms of indigestion and dyspepsia, which can contribute to feelings of nausea and discomfort.
Carminative property: Clove essential oil has a carminative effect, helping to release trapped gas in the digestive system and relieve bloating and discomfort that can exacerbate nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in eugenol, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate nausea and vomiting.
Rhyzomes: Contain sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to exhibit anti-nausea properties by reducing gastric contractions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of syzygium aromaticum in the early morning for maximum oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves in a shaded area for 3-5 days or until they reach 10% moisture level.
Steam distill the dried leaves using a glass still and 20 grams of leaves per liter of water.
Collect the essential oil from the condenser coil after 4 hours of distillation, resulting in approximately 2% yield.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or cotton cloth to remove any impurities before bottling.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat nausea with herbal essential oils is by inhaling peppermint oil, which helps to calm the stomach and reduce discomfort. Add a few drops of peppermint oil to a diffuser or mix with water and inhale the vapors. Ginger essential oil also works well, as it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate nausea. Use these oils in moderation and always dilute with a carrier oil before applying topically.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to nausea that are treated with herbal essential oils are digestive issues such as indigestion, bloating, and stomach discomfort. Peppermint, ginger, and fennel essential oils have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the stomach and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Additionally, these oils can also help to calm stress-related symptoms like anxiety and insomnia, which often manifest as nausea in some individuals.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with nausea because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe the digestive system. The oil's active compounds, including gingerols and shogaols, work to relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating nausea and vomiting. Additionally, the oil's calming effects on the nervous system can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for nausea. As a result, Zingiber officinale essential oils can provide quick and effective relief from nausea and associated discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Zingiberene: A sesquiterpene that helps alleviate nausea by inhibiting the contraction of smooth muscles in the stomach, thereby reducing vomiting and nausea.
Gingerol: A phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate nausea associated with digestive issues.

Shogaol: A phenolic compound that has a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to reduce nausea and vomiting by inhibiting the release of serotonin, a hormone involved in nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most used part due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which are responsible for the anti-inflammatory and anti-emetic properties.
Roots: Roots are often used as a substitute for rhyzomes, as they contain similar compounds that help alleviate nausea and other gastrointestinal issues.
Buds: Buds are used in some essential oil blends for their potential to reduce nausea and inflammation, although they may not be as effective as rhyzomes or roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature zingiber officinale rhizomes with a spade at dawn when moisture levels are optimal.
Steam distill the rhizomes in a copper still using 20 pounds of plant material per hour.
Collect the resulting distillate in a condenser cooled with ice water to prevent oil degradation.
Separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a separator or decantation over a period of 2 hours.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass container lined with a silica gel packet at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare essential oils helps with nausea because of its carminative and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as anethole and fenchone, work together to soothe the digestive system and reduce spasms in the stomach muscles. This helps to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness, morning sickness, and other gastrointestinal issues. Additionally, Foeniculum vulgare's natural sweetness and aroma can also help to calm the mind and body, promoting a sense of well-being and relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: Anethole has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe the stomach and reduce nausea by relaxing the digestive muscles.
Fenchone: Fenchone has a sedative effect on the central nervous system, which can help to alleviate nausea by reducing anxiety and stress-related symptoms.
Fenchyl acetate: Fenchyl acetate has carminative properties, which help to reduce gas and bloating in the stomach, thereby relieving nausea and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Foeniculum vulgare are commonly used to make essential oils for nausea due to their high concentration of compounds like anethole, which has anti-nausea properties.
Seeds: Foeniculum vulgare seeds are another major source of essential oils for nausea, as they contain a significant amount of anethole, which is known for its anti-nausea effects.
Buds: The buds of Foeniculum vulgare, rich in essential oils, are also used to make essential oils for nausea, as they contain compounds like anethole, which help alleviate nausea and vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 pounds of foeniculum vulgare seeds and leaves when they are in full bloom and the seeds are mature.
Dry the harvested foeniculum vulgare in a single layer at room temperature for 2 to 3 weeks.
Steam distill 2 pounds of the dried foeniculum vulgare using a steam distillation apparatus for 2 hours.
Collect and separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a separator or a distillation apparatus for 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and store in dark glass bottles immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils helps with nausea because of its natural digestive properties and ability to soothe the stomach. The oil's main compound, menthol, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help calm the digestive system and reduce nausea. Additionally, mentha oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can also contribute to its nausea-relieving properties. This makes mentha x piperita essential oils a popular natural remedy for nausea and digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Menthone: Menthone acts as a natural anti-inflammatory and has a calming effect on the stomach, which helps alleviate nausea.
Menthol: Menthol has a soothing effect on the stomach lining, reduces inflammation, and helps in relieving digestive issues associated with nausea.
Limonene: Limonene exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which helps in reducing nausea by calming the digestive system and alleviating stomach discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part to make essential oils for nausea because they contain the highest concentration of menthol, a key compound responsible for its anti-nausea properties.
Stems: Stems are also used to make essential oils for nausea, as they contain menthol and other compounds that can help alleviate nausea and digestive issues.
Roots: The roots of Mentha x piperita are used to make essential oils for nausea, as they contain menthol and other compounds that can help soothe the stomach and alleviate nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and stems in the early morning to ensure highest essential oil content.
Steam distill the harvested plant material for 2 hours using a 1:10 plant material to water ratio to extract essential oils.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water through decantation or filtration to obtain the desired product.
Store the extracted essential oil in a clean dark glass bottle away from direct sunlight and heat sources for up to 6 months.
Use a gc-ms analyzer to test the essential oil's purity and chemical composition before labeling and distributing it as a finished product.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum essential oils helps with nausea because of its natural anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. The oil contains compounds like limonene and pinene, which have been shown to relax the stomach muscles and reduce spasms, alleviating nausea and vomiting. Additionally, the oil's antioxidant properties help to soothe the digestive system, reducing inflammation and promoting a sense of calm, making it a popular natural remedy for nausea and digestive issues. Its unique properties make it an effective solution for alleviating nausea.
remedy_constituents: Limonene: A terpene that helps alleviate nausea by stimulating digestion and relieving spasms in the gastrointestinal tract.
Pinene: A terpene that helps with nausea by reducing inflammation in the digestive system and promoting a sense of calmness.
Germacrene: A sesquiterpene that helps with nausea by possessing anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive system and reduce discomfort.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Piper nigrum seeds are used to make essential oils for nausea due to their high concentration of volatile oils, particularly piperine, which has anti-inflammatory properties.
Fruits: Piper nigrum fruits, also known as peppercorns, are used for essential oils to alleviate nausea as they contain essential oils that have a calming effect on the digestive system.
Leaves: Piper nigrum leaves are used to make essential oils for nausea due to their high content of flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh piper nigrum berries in large quantities typically around 1 kg for small-scale distillation.
Steam distill the harvested berries in a copper or stainless steel still at 100 degrees celsius for 3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a separate container with a capacity of 100 milliliters for small-scale distillation.
Filter the collected essential oil using activated charcoal to remove impurities and improve its quality and clarity.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with a tight-fitting lid in a cool place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils helps with nausea because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to improve blood flow to the stomach. This helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive discomfort, making it easier to manage nausea. The antioxidants present in Ginkgo biloba also work to neutralize free radicals that can cause nausea and digestive issues. As a result, Ginkgo biloba essential oils can help to calm the stomach and reduce the frequency and severity of nausea.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These help by stabilizing mast cell membranes, reducing inflammation, and inhibiting histamine release, which can contribute to nausea.
Ginkgolides: These help by blocking platelet-activating factor (PAF), a compound involved in inflammation and nausea, as well as reducing histamine release.
Bilobalide: This helps by modulating neurotransmitter activity, reducing anxiety and stress that can contribute to nausea.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are responsible for the medicinal properties of Ginkgo biloba.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds are used to create essential oils that help alleviate nausea and other digestive issues due to their high content of bilobalide, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: Ginkgo biloba bark is used to make essential oils that may help reduce nausea and other symptoms associated with nausea due to its content of flavonoids and terpenoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in the fall season when they are fully ripe.
Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves in a clean well-ventilated area for 1-2 weeks.
Steam distill 500 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in a distillation apparatus for 2 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from the water and solids.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oils helps with nausea because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the stomach. The oil's warm, spicy aroma also has a calming effect on the mind, helping to alleviate anxiety and stress that can contribute to nausea. Additionally, Cinnamomum verum has been traditionally used to aid digestion and relieve symptoms of motion sickness, making it a popular natural remedy for nausea and indigestion. Its therapeutic benefits make it a valuable addition to aromatherapy routines.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: Acts as a stomach soother and helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, thereby alleviating nausea.
Linalool: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to calm the digestive system and reduce nausea.
Eugenol: Possesses analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with nausea, while also promoting a sense of calmness.
remedy_parts: Barks: Cinnamon barks are commonly used for essential oils as they contain high levels of cinnamaldehyde, a compound that can help alleviate nausea.
Leaves: Cinnamon leaves are another key component for essential oils, providing a similar composition to the bark and offering relief from nausea.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Cinnamomum verum, also known as cinnamon roots, are used to produce essential oils that can help soothe nausea due to their cinnamaldehyde content.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cinnamomum verum leaves and twigs from mature trees during peak oil production in early morning.
Steam distill the harvested cinnamomum verum material in a 10:1 water-to-plant ratio at 100°c for 3 hours.
Collect the distilled oil and separate it from the water using a series of separators and filters.
Store the cinnamomum verum essential oil in a dark glass bottle at 4°c to prevent degradation and oxidation.
Age the cinnamomum verum essential oil for at least 30 days before bottling to allow the flavors to mature and stabilize.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis essential oils helps with nausea because of its unique ability to calm the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the stomach. The oils contain compounds like quinine, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract and alleviate nausea. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis has a balancing effect on the body's natural rhythms, helping to regulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of nausea, making it a popular natural remedy for digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: A natural antipyretic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate nausea by reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms associated with fever.
Quinidine: A naturally occurring alkaloid that has a sedative effect on the stomach, helping to soothe nausea and reduce vomiting.
Quinovic Acid Glycoside: A compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nausea by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Cinchona officinalis are rich in quinine, a natural compound that helps alleviate nausea and other symptoms associated with gastrointestinal issues.
Barks: The bark of Cinchona officinalis contains quinine and other compounds that contribute to its antiemetic properties, making it effective in treating nausea and vomiting.
Roots: The roots of Cinchona officinalis also contain quinine, which is used to make essential oils that help alleviate nausea, fever, and other health conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cinchona officinalis leaves in the early morning to ensure optimal alkaloid content.
Dry the leaves in a well-ventilated area at a temperature of 35-40°c for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried leaves in a 2:1 ratio of leaves to water for 2-3 hours.
Separate and decant the essential oil from the distillate using a separator or still.
Filter the essential oil through activated charcoal to remove impurities and improve clarity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ammi majus essential oils helps with nausea because of its natural anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. The oil's chemical composition, particularly its sesquiterpene content, is known to have a soothing effect on the digestive system, reducing spasms and cramping that can lead to nausea. Additionally, Ammi majus oil's ability to regulate gut motility and alleviate digestive discomfort makes it a popular natural remedy for nausea and other gastrointestinal issues, providing relief from symptoms and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Carvone: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate nausea by reducing inflammation in the stomach and intestines.
Dihydrocarvone: It exhibits anti-emetic properties, helping to relieve nausea by blocking the signals that trigger vomiting.
Sabinene: It has a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing nausea and vomiting by soothing the stomach and intestines.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ammi majus contain essential oils that help alleviate nausea due to their carminative and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Ammi majus are used in aromatherapy to relieve nausea and promote digestive health due to their calming and soothing effects.
Roots: The roots of Ammi majus contain essential oils that have been traditionally used to treat nausea and other gastrointestinal issues due to their sedative and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh flowers in the morning when dew is still present for maximum oil content.
Steam distill the flowers at 100-105°c temperature for 2-3 hours to separate oil.
Collect the distilled oil and filter it through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities.
Store the essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids in a cool place.
Label the bottles with the date and content of the oil for future reference.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils helps with nausea because of its natural anti-inflammatory and calming properties. The oil, extracted from the licorice root, has been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues and alleviate stomach discomfort. Its active compounds, such as glycyrrhizin, work to balance the body's pH levels and regulate digestive enzymes, effectively reducing nausea and vomiting. Additionally, its sweet and soothing aroma can also help to calm the mind and body, promoting relaxation and reducing feelings of queasiness.
remedy_constituents: Terpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate nausea by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and protecting cells from damage.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils have been shown to have anti-emetic properties, helping to reduce nausea and vomiting by modulating neurotransmitters and improving gut health.

Saponins: Saponins have anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic properties, which can help to relieve nausea by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and preventing spasms that can contribute to nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are commonly used to make essential oils for nausea due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate nausea and vomiting.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are also used to make essential oils for nausea due to their ability to promote digestion and alleviate symptoms of nausea and indigestion.
Buds: The buds of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make essential oils for nausea due to their high content of flavonoids and other compounds that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of the plant's roots and dry them in a warm place for 2 weeks to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Steam distill the dried roots for 8 hours using a 1:5 plant to water ratio to extract the oil.
Collect and filter the oil to remove any impurities and sediment using a paper filter.
Store the oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to prevent light and air exposure.
Use the oil within 6 months of distillation to ensure its quality and potency remain intact.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper methysticum essential oils helps with nausea because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to nausea. The oil also has a sedative effect, which can help to calm the mind and body, reducing feelings of queasiness and discomfort. Additionally, Piper methysticum oil has been traditionally used to calm the digestive system and promote relaxation, making it a natural remedy for nausea relief.
remedy_constituents: Isorhynchophylline: This alkaloid has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate nausea by reducing inflammation in the digestive system.
Isoorientin: This flavonoid has antioxidant properties, which can help protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress and reduce nausea associated with inflammation.
Kaurenoic acid: This diterpenoid has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the muscles in the digestive system and reduce nausea.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves of Piper methysticum are commonly used to make essential oils due to their high concentration of active compounds that have anti-nausea properties.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Piper methysticum are utilized for essential oil production because they contain a high amount of kavalactones, which are known to alleviate nausea and vomiting.

Barks: Barks of Piper methysticum are also used for essential oil production, as they contain compounds that have a calming effect on the stomach, helping to reduce nausea and other gastrointestinal issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh piper methysticum leaves and stems with a sharp gardening knife in early morning.
Steam distill the harvested piper methysticum leaves and stems at 100c for 2 hours.
Collect the distilled vapor and condense it into liquid using a condenser coil in 30 minutes.
Filter the liquid essential oil using a paper filter to remove impurities in 10 minutes.
Store the final product in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool place.
